pattern: Each level is sorted independently, and the minimum number of value swaps to sort a permutation equals its size minus the number of cycles in the mapping from current to sorted positions.
hints: Swaps on one level never help another, so collect each level's values and solve them separately.
Sorting a sequence by swaps costs (number of elements) minus (number of cycles) in the permutation that takes current positions to their sorted positions.
For each level, map every value to the index it should occupy when sorted, then count cycles in that mapping; sum (length - cycles) over all levels.

edge_inputs.description: every level needs a few swaps
a single long cycle on one level

stress.description: random complete tree with a permutation of distinct values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    vals = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(vals)
    return [vals]
stress.note: dense array form is a valid level-order complete tree; values are distinct
